name: Arunachal Pradesh (अरुणाचल प्रदेश / Doni Polo)
summary: Arunachal Pradesh (अरुणाचल प्रदेश in Hindi, meaning 'Land of Dawn-Lit Mountains' / Doni Polo in Tani languages) is a North-eastern Indian Himalayan mixed common-law/multi-tribal-customary-law constituent state of the Indian Republic — structurally distinctive globally as the only Indian state operating constitutional Article 371-H special-status framework providing for special responsibility of the Governor regarding law and order (notably allowing the Governor to exercise individual judgment after consultation with the Council of Ministers — distinct from the more extensive customary-law-protection Article 371-A/G/F frameworks), as the central jurisdiction of the Sino-Indian border dispute with the People's Republic of China (which claims most of Arunachal Pradesh as 'Southern Tibet' / Zangnan and renamed places via 2017+ Chinese-name-publication campaigns), and as the most ethnically diverse Indian state with 26 major tribes (including Adi, Apatani, Galo, Monpa, Nyishi, Tagin, Tani, and 19 other recognised tribes) speaking 90+ distinct languages, the most multi-tribal-population Indian state by tribe count. Arunachal Pradesh was the North-East Frontier Agency (NEFA) under federal administration until 1972, then a Union Territory until 1987, then formed as the 24th state of India on 20 February 1987 under the Constitution (Fifty-Fifth Amendment) Act 1986 and the State of Arunachal Pradesh Act 1986. Arunachal Pradesh comprises ~83,743 km² with a population of ~1.4 million. Family-law framework operates under a dual federal Indian common-law + multi-tribal-customary-law framework with tribal customary law applied via traditional village councils (Kebang, Buliang, Bango) operating under Inner Line Permit (ILP) framework restricting non-resident access. Parental authority and child custody operate under tribal customary law for tribal persons subject to Indian general framework. The Gauhati High Court (Itanagar Permanent Bench) is the apex domestic appellate court; final appellate jurisdiction lies with the Supreme Court of India. Arunachal Pradesh is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label. India is a Hague Convention 1980 signatory but not a party — Arunachal Pradesh is governed by the Indian non-ratification status.

statutory_framework.citation: Constitution of India Article 371-H (Arunachal Pradesh Special Provisions)
statutory_framework.title: Constitution of India Article 371-H
statutory_framework.relevance: Indian Constitutional Article inserted by Constitution (Fifty-Fifth Amendment) Act 1986 establishing special provisions for Arunachal Pradesh — providing for special responsibility of the Governor regarding law and order with individual judgment authority after consultation with Council of Ministers.

statutory_framework.title: State of Arunachal Pradesh Act
statutory_framework.relevance: Indian Federal Act of 1986 establishing the State of Arunachal Pradesh effective 20 February 1987.

statutory_framework.citation: Bengal Eastern Frontier Regulation 1873 (Inner Line Permit framework)
statutory_framework.title: Bengal Eastern Frontier Regulation
statutory_framework.relevance: Foundational Inner Line Permit (ILP) framework restricting non-resident access to Arunachal Pradesh — preserved continuously since British colonial framework.

statutory_framework.title: Constitution 55th Amendment Act
statutory_framework.relevance: Indian Constitutional Amendment Act of 1986 inserting Article 371-H providing special Governor responsibility framework for Arunachal Pradesh.

apex_courts.name: Gauhati High Court (Itanagar Permanent Bench)
apex_courts.role: Apex domestic appellate court for civil and criminal matters from Arunachal Pradesh.

apex_courts.name: Supreme Court of India
apex_courts.role: Final appellate court for civil and criminal matters from Arunachal Pradesh.

apex_courts.name: Traditional village councils (Kebang, Buliang, Bango)
apex_courts.role: Tribal customary courts operating under tribal customary law and ILP framework.

professional_regulators.name: Indian Rehabilitation Council of India (RCI)
professional_regulators.role: Indian Federal professional regulatory framework applicable in Arunachal Pradesh.

anonymisation_convention: Arunachal Pradesh family-court decisions are anonymised per Indian court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Bengal Eastern Frontier Regulation + ILP-framework framework
key_developments.description: Bengal Eastern Frontier Regulation 1873 establishing foundational Inner Line Permit (ILP) framework restricting non-resident access to North-eastern frontier territories including future Arunachal Pradesh — substantively distinctive globally only-modern-Indian-state-with-continuous-150-year-ILP-framework framework. Foundational substantive ILP-framework persisting through 21st century affecting subsequent constitutional-administrative-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Simla Convention + McMahon-Line + Tibet-British-India-+-Chinese-non-signing framework
key_developments.description: Simla Convention 3 July 1914 establishing McMahon Line as Indo-Tibetan border — substantively distinctive globally Indo-Tibetan-border-McMahon-Line-+-Chinese-non-signing framework. Foundational substantive Sino-Indian-border-dispute framework affecting subsequent 1962 Sino-Indian War and Chinese Zangnan-claim framework.

key_developments.title: Sino-Indian War + Arunachal-Pradesh-as-central-front framework
key_developments.description: Sino-Indian War October-November 1962 — substantively distinctive Himalayan Arunachal-Pradesh-then-NEFA-as-central-front-in-the-conflict-with-PRC framework. Substantive demographic and constitutional-democratic-trajectory framework affecting subsequent constitutional-administrative-trajectory and continuing Sino-Indian border-dispute framework.

key_developments.title: NEFA + Union-Territory-of-Arunachal-Pradesh framework
key_developments.description: North-East Frontier Agency (NEFA) reorganised as Union Territory of Arunachal Pradesh 21 January 1972 — substantively significant Himalayan post-NEFA-Union-Territory framework. Foundational pre-1987-statehood framework affecting subsequent constitutional-administrative-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Constitution 55th Amendment + Article-371-H-Governor-special-responsibility framework
key_developments.description: Constitution (Fifty-Fifth Amendment) Act 1986 inserting Article 371-H providing special Governor responsibility framework regarding law and order — substantively distinctive globally only-Indian-state-operating-Article-371-H-Governor-special-responsibility framework. Substantive constitutional-democratic-trajectory framework affecting subsequent reform-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Arunachal Pradesh State + 24th-Indian-state framework
key_developments.description: Arunachal Pradesh formed as 24th state of India 20 February 1987 under the State of Arunachal Pradesh Act 1986 — substantively distinctive Himalayan 24th-Indian-state-via-Union-Territory-to-state framework. Foundational substantive 39-year Arunachal-Pradesh-statehood framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: India ratifies UN Convention on the Rights of the Child + Arunachal-Pradesh-applicability framework
key_developments.description: India ratified UNCRC 11 December 1992 with applicability to Arunachal Pradesh — establishing best-interests-of-the-child substantive doctrine within Indian-derivative-common-law framework + multi-tribal-customary-law co-applicability via traditional village councils (Kebang, Buliang, Bango).

key_developments.title: Chinese Zangnan place-naming campaign + PRC-Indian-border-dispute-escalation framework
key_developments.description: PRC commenced systematic publication of Chinese names for Arunachal Pradesh places (~30 places in 2017 list, expanded in 2021 and 2024) — substantively distinctive globally only-modern-major-sub-national-territorial-dispute-pursued-via-systematic-place-naming framework. Substantive Sino-Indian-border-dispute escalation framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Galwan Valley confrontation + Sino-Indian-LAC-tension + Arunachal-Pradesh-strategic-position framework
key_developments.description: Galwan Valley confrontation 15-16 June 2020 substantively reshaping Sino-Indian-LAC-tension framework — substantively distinctive Himalayan Arunachal-Pradesh-strategic-position-+-Galwan-Valley-Ladakh-context framework. Subsequent 2022 + 2024 China-India clashes affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Pema Khandu government framework + APSCW institutional framework + 26-tribe demographic framework
key_developments.description: Pema Khandu (BJP) Chief Ministership 2016-onwards framework consolidation through 2023. Arunachal Pradesh State Commission for Women (APSCW) institutional position addressing PA-related issues within tribal-customary-law + Hindu Marriage Act framework. 26-tribe demographic framework (Adi, Apatani, Nyishi, Galo, Tagin, Mishmi, etc.) substantively significant institutional context. Substantively significant Northeast Indian state institutional jurisprudential development.

key_developments.title: Gauhati High Court Itanagar Permanent Bench + Supreme Court + traditional village councils — best-interests-of-the-child + tribal-customary-law substantive register + continuing-Article-371-H-+-Sino-Indian-border-dispute framework
key_developments.description: Gauhati High Court (Itanagar Permanent Bench), Supreme Court of India, and traditional village councils (Kebang, Buliang, Bango) continue to develop best-interests-of-the-child + tribal-customary-law jurisprudence under Article 371-H + Sixth Schedule + Bengal Eastern Frontier Regulation 1873 ILP framework in custody disputes within continuing Article 371-H Governor-special-responsibility-+-Sino-Indian-border-dispute context. Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

structural_findings: Arunachal Pradesh operates a mixed federal Indian common-law + multi-tribal-customary-law framework — places Arunachal Pradesh in the North-eastern Indian Indigenous-tribal-customary-law-protected cluster.

Only Indian state operating constitutional Article 371-H special-status framework providing for special responsibility of the Governor regarding law and order is structurally distinctive globally — distinct from more extensive customary-law-protection Article 371-A/G/F frameworks.
Central jurisdiction of Sino-Indian border dispute with PRC claiming most of Arunachal Pradesh as 'Southern Tibet'/Zangnan is structurally distinctive globally.

PRC 2017+ Chinese-name-publication campaigns escalating border dispute via place-naming framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern major sub-national territorial dispute pursued via systematic place-naming framework.
Most ethnically diverse Indian state with 26 major tribes speaking 90+ distinct languages is structurally distinctive globally — most multi-tribal-population Indian state by tribe count.
Inner Line Permit (ILP) framework restricting non-resident access since 1873 is structurally distinctive globally — only modern Indian state with continuous 150+-year ILP framework.
Traditional village councils (Kebang, Buliang, Bango) framework operating under ILP + Article 371-H protection is structurally distinctive within Indian state framework.
editorial_notes: Arunachal Pradesh jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 6 to 10 key_developments with full Bengal-Eastern-Frontier-Regulation-1873-to-continuing-Article-371-H-+-Sino-Indian-border-dispute trajectory: 1873-Bengal-Eastern-Frontier-Regulation-+-ILP + 1914-Simla-Convention-+-McMahon-Line + 1962-Sino-Indian-War + 1972-NEFA-+-Union-Territory + 1986-Constitution-55th-Amendment-+-Article-371-H + 1987-Arunachal-Pradesh-State-established + 1992-India-UNCRC-ratification + 2017-Chinese-Zangnan-place-naming-campaign + 2020-Galwan-Valley-confrontation + 2024-Gauhati-High-Court-Itanagar-+-Supreme-Court-+-traditional-village-councils.

Mixed federal Indian common-law/multi-tribal-customary-law North-eastern Indian Himalayan Indian-state (Indian Constitution Article 371-H + Constitution 55th Amendment Act 1986 + State of Arunachal Pradesh Act 1986 + Bengal Eastern Frontier Regulation 1873 Inner Line Permit + traditional village councils Kebang/Buliang/Bango + Sino-Indian border dispute + PRC 2017+ Chinese-name-publication campaigns + Indian non-Hague-1980). Only Indian state operating Article 371-H Governor special-responsibility framework globally + central jurisdiction of Sino-Indian border dispute with PRC claiming most of Arunachal Pradesh as Southern Tibet/Zangnan + only modern major sub-national territorial dispute pursued via systematic place-naming framework + most ethnically diverse Indian state with 26 major tribes speaking 90+ languages + only modern Indian state with continuous 150+-year ILP framework.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive best-interests-of-the-child + tribal-customary-law analysis under Article 371-H + Sixth Schedule + Bengal Eastern Frontier Regulation 1873 ILP framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins North-eastern-Indian + Himalayan + mixed-Indian-common-law/multi-tribal-customary-law + Indian-state-cluster + Article-371-H-Governor-special-responsibility-framework-globally-distinctive + Sino-Indian-border-dispute-Southern-Tibet-Zangnan-globally-distinctive + PRC-Chinese-name-publication-place-naming-framework-2017-2021-2024-globally-distinctive + 26-major-tribes-90-plus-languages-most-ethnically-diverse-Indian-state-globally-distinctive + Bengal-Eastern-Frontier-Regulation-1873-ILP-150-year-framework + Kebang-Buliang-Bango-traditional-village-councils + Simla-Convention-1914-McMahon-Line + Sino-Indian-War-1962-+-Galwan-Valley-2020 + NEFA-1972-+-State-1987 + Constitution-55th-Amendment-1986 + Article-371-cluster-with-Nagaland-Mizoram-Sikkim + Indian-non-Hague-1980 clusters within the corpus.
